evals.prompt: What's working in our niche over the last 30 days? We're a B2B SaaS for developer productivity. Competitors are Linear, Height, and Shortcut.
evals.expected_output: A plan confirming seed set (competitor handles, niche keywords), a surface mix biased to YouTube long-form / HN / GitHub / blogs (not TikTok), a cost estimate via apify-plan-signal-collection, and only after confirmation, a ranked brief with themes, formats, breakout creators, outlier posts, and routed handoffs into content-strategy / social-content / competitor-alternatives.
assertions: Calls apify-plan-signal-collection before apify-run-signal-collection-target
Proposes at most 3 surfaces and justifies each for a B2B dev niche
Does NOT default to TikTok/Instagram for this niche
Includes a confirmation step with estimated cost before running paid scrapes
Deliverable ranks clusters rather than listing raw posts
Routes at least one theme into a named downstream skill (content-strategy, social-content, competitor-alternatives, etc.)

evals.prompt: I don't have budget for apify this month but I still want to see what's trending in the indie hacker / bootstrapped SaaS community.
evals.expected_output: A free-tier-only pass that uses Reddit public JSON, HN Algolia, GitHub, and direct WebFetch on relevant blogs. No apify calls. The deliverable explicitly states the tier used and flags that cross-surface dedupe is limited without paid sources.
assertions: Does NOT call apify-plan-signal-collection or any apify-* tool
Uses Reddit public JSON (.json endpoint) for relevant subreddits
Uses HN Algolia API or GitHub for dev-adjacent signal
Deliverable says 'free tier' or equivalent and explains the trade-off
Does not fabricate engagement numbers for missing sources

evals.prompt: Pull a last30days scan for our consumer creator-tool niche. We compete with CapCut and Riverside. Focus on TikTok and Instagram.
evals.expected_output: A plan with apify-plan-signal-collection covering TikTok + Instagram for both competitor handles and niche hashtags, a cost confirmation, ranked themes/formats normalized against creator baseline, per-author cap applied, cross-surface dedupe between TikTok and Reels, and breakout creator detection.
assertions: Uses both TikTok and Instagram apify surfaces
Seeds include BOTH competitor handles AND niche keyword/hashtag searches
Deliverable normalizes engagement per surface (does not compare raw TikTok views to Instagram plays)
Deliverable applies a per-author cap (same creator does not occupy >2 top slots)
Deliverable cross-surface dedupes cross-posted content between TikTok and Reels
Includes breakout-creator section with baseline-vs-recent lift

evals.prompt: Can you pull all the top Reddit posts from r/SaaS, r/startups, and r/entrepreneur over the last 30 days with full comment threads and author histories?
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges that apify Reddit scraping at scale is not wired in this codebase. Offers Tier 1 fallback using Reddit public JSON (top.json?t=month) for the three subreddits. States limitation (no full comment-tree scraping without a Reddit actor) and flags as follow-up work. Does NOT fabricate a Reddit scrape or invoke the wrong actor.
assertions: Explicitly says Reddit-at-scale apify scraping is not currently wired
Falls back to Reddit public JSON for the three subreddits
Does NOT call apify-run-actor with a made-up Reddit actor id
Notes the limitation as a follow-up improvement rather than claiming completion

evals.prompt: We just did a last30days scan and the themes were strong. Can you turn this into something we keep running automatically?
evals.expected_output: Hands the durable patterns from the one-off scan into external-signal-monitoring with a proposed monitor scope, cadence, and routing. Clearly distinguishes which clusters are durable enough to monitor (multi-creator, multi-surface, lift-sustained) versus which were one-off outliers that should NOT become recurring monitors.
assertions: Routes the follow-up into external-signal-monitoring, not a new last30days-research run
Filters which clusters are monitor-worthy (multi-creator + durable lift) vs one-off
Proposes a specific cadence (e.g., weekly)
Does not simply rerun the same ad hoc scan
